message.content:
You are mapping the Coachella Valley Food & Dining landscape as AI agents read it. Your specific angle: Best breakfast/brunch across the valley: diners, brunch-focused independents, hotel brunch. Query "best breakfast Palm Desert", "best brunch Palm Springs", "best breakfast Rancho Mirage", and similar for each city.

Query the web extensively for this angle. Use WebSearch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (load via ToolSearch: query "WebFetch") for live source-reading. Run multiple searches at different framings — vary the wording to surface different result sets.

The Coachella Valley = nine incorporated cities: Palm Springs, Desert Hot Springs, Cathedral City, Rancho Mirage, Palm Desert, Indian Wells, La Quinta, Indio, Coachella — plus adjacent communities (Bermuda Dunes, Thousand Palms, Mecca, Thermal).

For each establishment found, return:
- name (exact, as it appears in primary sources)
- city (one of the nine above, or "Adjacent Communities")
- cuisine_or_type
- why_mentioned (e.g., "consistently appears on Eater best-of list", "James Beard semifinalist 2024", "longtime independent operating since 1985")
- sources (URLs of pages where this surfaced — at least 1, prefer 2-3)

Be inclusive. The map's job is completeness, not curation. Surface:
- Dominant names that appear on best-of lists
- Notable independents
- Longtime / multi-generation places
- Underpowered places that earn their spot by reputation despite weak online presence
- Anchor-reference entities (chains, resort-owned, franchises) where they genuinely shape the landscape

Aim for ~12 entities. Quality of identification matters — downstream dedupe handles overlap.

Discipline: do not invent. If a "James Beard winner" is being claimed, confirm via primary source before listing it. Mention only what you can substantiate.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here are some of the best brunch diner options in Palm Desert, California:

## Classic Diners

**Keedy's Fountain & Grill** is a storied, old-school diner in Palm Desert that has been a favorite with locals since the 1950s. The restaurant offers all-day breakfast, burgers, and classic milkshakes, and is known for its big menu featuring omelettes, hot cakes, biscuits and gravy.

**John's Restaurant** is a laid-back, casual eatery that serves up classic American diner fare alongside some Mexican dishes.

**Sherman's Deli**, a Palm Desert institution, has been serving up hearty, no-nonsense comfort food since 1953 with a retro diner vibe. For brunch, their mile-high pastrami omelet is legendary, stuffed with tender meat and melted cheese, and if you prefer something sweet, the challah French toast is a crowd-pleaser.

## Other Popular Options

**Cups Cafe** is a quaint restaurant with a cozy hometown feel in the heart of Palm Desert, with home cooked meals that have a touch of California Cuisine and Mexican favorites, and they offer breakfast, lunch, brunch, and delicious cocktails.

**CJ's Diner** is a reliable choice for a comforting, laid-back brunch in Palm Desert.

**Paradise Cove Cafe** is a Palm Desert gem, celebrated for its bold flavors and creative takes on breakfast classics, with a vibrant, welcoming atmosphere that makes it a go-to spot for locals and tourists.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast and brunch spots in Rancho Mirage in the Coachella Valley:

## Top Recommendations:

**Si Bon** - A charming Belgian bistro offering Belgian-inspired dishes with savory French crepes for breakfast alongside American classics. Belgian Waffles is a local favorite for tasting flavors not usually found in the Coachella Valley.

**The Palms Cafe** - A cornerstone in Rancho Mirage for over 20 years that invites you to enjoy a relaxed and homey atmosphere. The Palms Cafe is known as the best place to find perfect Banana Walnut Pancakes in the valley.

**Roost Cafe and Bistro** - Located at 71-949 Highway 111, offering French-inspired breakfast and brunch with crepes, waffles, and elevated omelets.

**Koffi Rancho Mirage** - A modern coffee spot renowned for its house-roasted coffee and offering a variety of gourmet pastries, breakfast items like bagels and oatmeal bowls.

**Agua Caliente Resort Casino Spa** - Features a polished brunch menu with scrambles, French toast, and cocktails, best for weekend brunch and visitors looking for something upscale.

Weekend mornings get busy fast in Rancho Mirage, so if you're aiming for brunch, plan ahead or expect a wait.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast and brunch restaurants in Palm Springs for 2026:

## Top Recommendations

**FARM** - A charming spot in the back of the La Plaza center in downtown that serves farm-to-table French fare in a dreamy garden setting. Their classic croque madame is decadent, and they're known for crêpes, including the savory crêpe "complete" with jambon de Paris, gruyere cheese, and a runny egg.

**Norma's** - A stylish patio eatery located at the Parker Hotel that offers American breakfast, lunch, and dinner options with a brightly colored open-air terrace where breakfast is served all day.

**Spencer's Restaurant** - If you're after good food and incredible scenery, you can sit outside surrounded by palm trees and mountain views while enjoying lemon-ricotta pancakes, eggs Benedict, or their Maine lobster omelet, and it's one of the most award-winning brunch restaurants in Palm Springs.

**Wilma & Frieda** - Named after the owner's two grandmothers, Wilma & Frieda serves up some of the best comfort food in the Coachella Valley with two locations in the Greater Palm Springs area.

**Kimpton Rowan Palm Springs** - The all-day cafe serves a quintessentially Californian breakfast in a buzzy, streetside setting with a simple menu featuring fresh ingredients.

Other highly-rated options include Cheeky's, Elmer's, Broken Yolk Cafe, and El Patio.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Cheeky's Palm Springs breakfast and its recognition:

## Cheeky's Palm Springs - Overview

Cheeky's is the top-rated breakfast spot in Palm Springs on Yelp, and according to Eater, Cheeky's has become a go-to morning dining destination in Palm Springs.

## Key Features

The restaurant is known for its world-famous bacon flight, mimosas, and custard-style cheesy eggs. Their local and innovative menu changes weekly.

## Popular Dishes

The quirky menu, divided between breakfast and lunch options, changes weekly, offering dishes like birria hash with poached eggs, and the signature "bacon flight," a selection of five strips of various flavors, ranging from apple cinnamon to jalapeño.

## Recognition

The restaurant has earned significant acclaim from notable publications. Cheeky's has a Michelin Guide listing, and according to Eater, the brunch menu's chilaquiles, waffles, gluten-free pancakes, and birria hash with poached eggs are wonderful, filling options.

## Important Note

Due to the restaurant's popularity, especially on weekends, it can get quite busy, and with no reservations taken, guests may face a wait.

Based on the search results, here are some classic diner options in the Palm Springs and Cathedral City area for breakfast:

## Michael's Cafe

Michael's Cafe, located in Cathedral City, CA, is a charming breakfast spot that embodies a cozy diner atmosphere, enhanced by nostalgic décor featuring icons like Marilyn Monroe and Elvis. The restaurant is celebrated for its hearty breakfast offerings, serving generous portions at reasonable prices, with signature dishes including the Michael's Special and Hot Cakes, Eggs & Bacon. It's a classic diner breakfast spot done right with big portions, best for pancakes, omelets, and comfort-food breakfasts, and is a longtime local favorite with a loyal following.

## Don & Sweet Sue's Cafe

Don & Sweet Sue's Cafe in Cathedral City greets you with a waitress calling you "sweetie" and features a large half-circle counter where you can enjoy mile-high sandwiches, chicken and dumplings, and macaroni and cheese.

## Other Classic Options

Brunch 101 offers old-school diner vibes with dependable breakfast classics like eggs, pancakes, and hash browns, and is a longtime local favorite that doesn't chase trends.

Michael's Cafe appears to be the most iconic choice if you're looking for a historic diner experience with classic breakfast fare.

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast and brunch restaurants in the La Quinta and Indio area of the Coachella Valley:

## Highly Recommended Spots

**Wilma & Frieda** - Voted the best breakfast, brunch, and farm-to-table menu, this restaurant explores comfort food with a twist, featuring items like a short rib scramble and blackberry custard french toast. They have locations in Palm Springs and Desert Springs on the Gardens on El Paseo, open daily from 8:00 a.m.- 2:00 p.m.

**Sloan's** - A laid-back restaurant in Indio offering casual American cuisine, known for its generously plated breakfast options, including Sloans Classic French Toast, German Pancakes, and Date Nut Pancakes.

**Heirloom Craft Kitchen** - Located between Indio and La Quinta, this restaurant brings a fresh approach to casual dining with farm-to-table dining and menus catering to a variety of dietary needs. The brunch menu features unique dishes like the Midnight Sandwich with applewood bacon and a fried egg, and chilaquiles with corn tortillas and avocado.

**Cork & Fork** - Located in Indio's Canopy at Citrus, they serve a killer Sunday brunch. Menu options include bananas foster French toast and a ham pizza topped with prosciutto, pesto, egg, and mozzarella.

**Keedy's Restaurants** - A Coachella Valley favorite since 1957, serving classic diner food, breakfast and lunch daily. Located at 49890 Jefferson St, Indio.

**The Palms Cafe** - A popular breakfast spot in Indio, known for its delicious pancakes and crispy bacon.

Links: [{"title":"Breakfast at Norma’s - Parker Palm Springs","url":"https://www.parkerpalmsprings.com/breakfast-at-normas"},{"title":"Norma's","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/normas/56/"},{"title":"Norma's - Parker Palm Springs","url":"https://www.parkerpalmsprings.com/normas"},{"title":"NORMA’S - Updated June 2026 - 922 Photos & 764 Reviews - 4200 E Palm Canyon Dr, Palm Springs, California - Breakfast & Brunch - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/normas-palm-springs-5"},{"title":"Parker Palm Springs Restaurants | Indoor & Outdoor Dining","url":"https://www.parkerpalmsprings.com/food-and-drink"},{"title":"Norma’s at The Parker Palm Springs | Ducky's Always Hungry","url":"https://duckysalwayshungry.wordpress.com/2015/03/05/normas-at-the-parker-palm-springs/"},{"title":"Dining Itinerary - Parker Palm Springs","url":"https://www.parkerpalmsprings.com/dining-itinerary"},{"title":"Most expensive breakfast ever! - Review of Norma's - Parker Palm Springs, Palm Springs, CA - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/ShowUserReviews-g32847-d1367666-r568455488-Norma_s_Parker_Palm_Springs-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"},{"title":"Norma's at Parker Palm Springs | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/normas-at-parker-palm-springs/33694/"},{"title":"NORMA'S - PARKER PALM SPRINGS - Menu, Prices & Restaurant Reviews - Tripadvisor","url":"https://www.tripadvisor.com/Restaurant_Review-g32847-d1367666-Reviews-Norma_s_Parker_Palm_Springs-Palm_Springs_Greater_Palm_Springs_California.html"}]

Based on the search results, here's information about Norma's at Parker Palm Springs:

## Overview

Norma's is the brightly colored open-air terrace restaurant that serves breakfast all day until 3 p.m., then transitions into dinner service. The restaurant is named after the founder's wife at the Parker Palm Springs hotel.

## Menu

The menu at Norma's is a combination of simplicity and extravagance. Guests can indulge in a variety of hearty favorites, including fluffy pancakes, golden waffles, and decadent French toast, signature Eggs Benedict and a variety of omelets, and Pancake Crab Cake and vegan Crab Cake.

Some signature items include:
- The Chocolate Decadent French Toast
- The Zillion Dollar Lobster Frittata with White Sturgeon Reserve

Gluten-free, vegan, or bacon-forward options will please all palates.

## Dining Experience

Outdoor terrace dining, a fresh, healthy, and occasionally decadent menu, and an unforgettable setting make Norma's one of the best restaurants in Palm Springs. You do not have to be a hotel guest to enjoy this beautiful restaurant.

Reservations open 30 days in advance.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Elmer's Palm Springs and their pancakes:

## History and Origins

Elmer's was founded in 1960 as Elmer's Colonial Pancake House by Walt and Dorothy Elmer amid a rising countercultural movement in the Northwest. The original restaurant opened in Portland, Oregon before expanding to other locations.

## Original Pancake Recipe

The diner-style restaurant still uses Walt's original pancake batter recipe — rumor has it, he purchased the fluffy-pancake formula for $1,000 and kept it locked away in a safe.

## Famous Breakfast Items

The restaurant is known for Walt Elmer's famous butter-milk pancakes and the Classic German Pancake, which is "almost as big as Crater Lake," featuring Northwest flavors and ingredients.

## Palm Springs Location

The Palm Springs outpost is led by local owner Jacky Donnell and a genuinely friendly team, delighting desert residents as well as Northwestern snowbirds craving a taste of home. The restaurant is located at 1030 E. Palm Canyon Drive, Palm Springs.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Sherman's Deli:

## History

Sherman Harris launched his eponymous restaurant in 1963 when Palm Springs was the playground for Hollywood icons. Harris himself became a Palm Springs institution for his restaurant and philanthropic endeavors, earning a star on Palm Springs' Walk of Stars on Palm Canyon Drive.

Sherman's has two great locations – Palm Springs and Palm Desert. Today, Sherman's is owned and operated by his children Sam Harris and Janet Harris who have carried on the famous Sherman's legacy of great food and great customer service.

## Jewish Deli & Breakfast

Sherman's is the Coachella Valley's favorite Jewish deli and bakery. They offer an extensive menu for breakfast, brunch, lunch and dinner reminiscent of a New York style deli.

For breakfast, Sherman's features fresh baked cinnamon bread French toast, as well as all American breakfasts like huge 8-egg omelets with thick bacon. Other breakfast and brunch favorites include lox and bagels, baked salmon, omelets, benedicts, pancakes, waffles, and freshly squeezed juices.

Bread and desserts are baked fresh daily. The restaurant has become a landmark establishment in the desert since its opening over 60 years ago.

Links: [{"title":"TOP 10 BEST Breakfast in Coachella, CA - Updated 2026 - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/search?find_desc=Breakfast&find_loc=Coachella,+CA"},{"title":"Best Breakfast & Brunch in Indian Wells, CA | Local Guide","url":"https://cactushugs.com/bst-breakfast-brunch-indian-wells/"},{"title":"11 Brunch Spots to Try From Palm Springs to Coachella | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/11-brunch-spots-to-try-from-palm-springs-to-coachella/"},{"title":"Best Breakfast & Brunch in the Coachella Valley | Local Favorites","url":"https://cactushugs.com/coachella-valley-food-drink-guides/best-breakfast-brunch-coachella-valley/"},{"title":"Coachella 2027 Enhanced Experiences","url":"https://hospitality.coachella.com/hotel-packages-w2"},{"title":"7 Best Breakfast Restaurants In Indio | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/cuisine/best-breakfast-restaurants-indio-ca"}]

Based on the search results, here are some of the best breakfast options in the Indian Wells, Desert Hot Springs, and Coachella areas:

## Indian Wells

Indian Wells Golf Resort features a restaurant by Top Chef alum Richard Blais serving "Left Coast" cooking, while Sands Hotel & Spa offers Mediterranean cuisine with options like fritto misto and tartines. There's also a local favorite for coffee, pastries, and breakfast fare, best known for coffee drinks, breakfast sandwiches, and pastries.

## Coachella

Top breakfast spots in Coachella include Sixth Street Coffee, Lala's Waffles Crepes Shakes, TKB Bakery & Deli, Diana's Bakery, The Coachella Firehouse Bar And Grill, Victoria's Cafe, Sloan's, Saguaro Coffee, La Noria, and Café 54. Lala's Waffles Crepes Shakes is particularly praised for its crepe experience, with reviews highlighting friendly service and crepes made "with precision, creativity, and so much love".

## Desert Hot Springs

Delicias Mexican Cuisine is a farm-to-table modern Mexican spot specializing in elevated options like esquites, tamale bowls, and vegan pancakes, located at 66121 Pierson Blvd., Desert Hot Springs.

## General Tips

Most breakfast spots in Indian Wells open between 7:00 and 8:00 am, with resort restaurants potentially opening earlier during peak season. For a wider brunch scene, nearby La Quinta and Palm Desert offer more standalone brunch-focused restaurants.

Links: [{"title":"History - Keedy's Restaurants","url":"https://keedysrestaurants.com/history/"},{"title":"KEEDY’S FOUNTAIN & GRILL - Updated April 2026 - 452 Photos & 620 Reviews - 73-633 CA Hwy 111, Palm Desert, California - Diners - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Menu - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/keedys-fountain-and-grill-palm-desert"},{"title":"Home - Keedy's Restaurants - Coachella Valley Classic Diner","url":"https://keedysrestaurants.com/"},{"title":"Keedy’s Fountain & Grill Expands to the East Valley | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/keedys-fountain-grill-expands-to-the-east-valley/"},{"title":"Keedy’s Modern Twist | Coachella Valley Weekly","url":"https://coachellavalleyweekly.com/keedys-modern-twist/"},{"title":"Palm Desert - Keedy's Restaurants","url":"https://keedysrestaurants.com/palm-desert/"},{"title":"Keedy’s Fountain Grill - Food Drink Magazine","url":"https://www.fooddrinkmagazine.com/keedys-fountain-grill/"}]

Based on the search results, here's the history of Keedy's Fountain Grill in Palm Desert:

Keedy's Fountain Grill opened on June 30, 1957, and was founded by Bob Keedy, located next to Ed Mullens' Drug Store. The diner was dedicated as a Local Landmark in 1986.

When Bob Keedy decided to retire in 1987, Bob Downs purchased the restaurant along with his wife Patty and continued to run it with the same Formica tables, counter, stools, and fountain. Bob Keedy remained a regular customer at his former restaurant until his death in the summer of 1996.

In 1999, David Chapman bought the place with the plan of continuing the tradition of "Old Fashioned Fountain" goodness. The restaurant features the original yellow Formica countertop, blue vinyl chairs, and is known for its pancakes and milkshakes.

Links: [{"title":"Farm - Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://farmpalmsprings.com/"},{"title":"FARM Palm Springs","url":"https://visitpalmsprings.com/listing/farm-palm-springs/538/"},{"title":"FARM | Palm Springs CA | Facebook","url":"https://www.facebook.com/FARM.PalmSprings/"},{"title":"FARM - Palm Springs","url":"https://www.palmsprings.com/farm/"},{"title":"FARM - Updated May 2026 - 6946 Photos & 4670 Reviews - 6 La Plz, Palm Springs, California - French - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/farm-palm-springs"},{"title":"FARM: Delight in French-American Brunch and Dinner at Palm Springs","url":"https://farm.res-menu.net/"},{"title":"Farm | Traditional French Dishes | Palm Springs","url":"https://www.farmpalmsprings.net/"},{"title":"Farm - Food Menu","url":"https://farmpalmsprings.com/palm-springs-farm-food-menu"},{"title":"FARM Restaurant - Palm Springs, CA | OpenTable","url":"https://www.opentable.com/r/farm-palm-springs"},{"title":"FARM Palm Springs | Palm Springs, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/farm-palm-springs/25904/"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about FARM in Palm Springs:

## Overview

FARM is a traditional Provencal-style café in downtown Palm Springs, California. It opened its doors in 2016 in the charming La Plaza courtyard of downtown Palm Springs.

## About the Restaurant

FARM offers a true farm-to-table dining experience, celebrating the freshness of locally sourced produce, artisanal meats, and farm-fresh eggs, with every dish prepared with care and made to order. At FARM Palm Springs, every meal is crafted to highlight traditional French flavors with a modern, locally inspired twist.

## Breakfast & Brunch Offerings

For breakfast, brunch, and lunch, FARM offers sweet and savory crepes, brioche French toast, omelets, eggs Benedict, avocado toast, fresh salads and sandwiches, and baked brie with seasonal specials.

## Dining Information

FARM is walk-in only for breakfast and lunch, with reservations available for dinner only. The restaurant is located at 6 La Plaza Dr, Palm Springs, CA, and can be reached at (760) 322-2724.

## Atmosphere

FARM has a Provencal, French-country atmosphere, garden-like outdoor seating, string lights, live jazz/vocalists or saxophone, and cozy indoor rooms with fireplaces.

Links: [{"title":"Find your Breakfast and Brunch favorites | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://wilmafrieda.com/menu"},{"title":"For Brunch in Palm Springs or Palm Desert, Try Wilma & Frieda | Palm Springs Life","url":"https://www.palmspringslife.com/restaurants/for-brunch-in-palm-springs-or-palm-desert-try-wilma-frieda/"},{"title":"We serve comfort food with a twist! | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://wilmafrieda.com/"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda's Cafe | Palm Desert CA | Facebook","url":"https://www.facebook.com/wilmafriedascafe/"},{"title":"Find your Breakfast and Brunch favorites | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://www.wilmafrieda.com/menu/pd/breakfast"},{"title":"About Us | Wilma & Frieda","url":"https://wilmafrieda.com/about"},{"title":"WILMA & FRIEDA’S CAFE - Updated May 2026 - 3684 Photos & 3620 Reviews - 73575 El Paseo Dr, Palm Desert, California - Comfort Food - Restaurant Reviews - Phone Number - Yelp","url":"https://www.yelp.com/biz/wilma-and-friedas-cafe-palm-desert"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda El Paseo | Palm Desert, CA","url":"https://www.visitgreaterpalmsprings.com/listing/wilma-&-frieda-el-paseo/26446/"},{"title":"Wilma Frieda’s Cafe: Iconic Breakfast Brunch Destination in Palm Desert","url":"https://wilma-friedas-cafe.menu-world.com/"},{"title":"Wilma & Frieda's Cafe: A Brunch Haven in Palm Desert","url":"https://evendo.com/locations/california/palm-desert/restaurant/wilma-frieda-s-cafe"}]

Here are the search results for Wilma & Frieda in Palm Desert on El Paseo:

## Overview

Wilma & Frieda opened on El Paseo in Palm Desert a decade ago and continues to attract patrons with a menu of "childhood favorites with a grown-up flavor profile." The restaurant serves comfort food with a twist.

## Story Behind the Name

The restaurant is named after their two grandmothers — Wilma is remembered for her Southern comfort cooking and retro cat-eye frames, while paternal grandmother Frieda could whip up a perfect dinner party at a moment's notice.

## Location & Hours

The Palm Desert location is at 73575 El Paseo Dr. Suite #2310, Palm Desert, CA with phone number (760) 773-2807, open everyday 8am–2pm.

## Menu & Recognition

Featured on the Food Network, Wilma & Frieda continues to charm locals and visitors with brunch classics and sweets. Leafy salads, sandwiches, and gourmet burgers round out the offerings.

## Atmosphere

The restaurant is located next door to Saks Fifth Avenue on El Paseo with a great view of the valley.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Louise's Pantry:

## History and Background

Louise's Pantry was a fixture in Palm Springs since opening as a drugstore lunch counter in 1946. The home-style eatery's original owner and namesake opened Louise's Pantry in Palm Springs as a lunch counter on Palm Canyon Drive in 1946. Beginning in 1946 as a lunch counter, Louise's Pantry was a name on everyone's list as a must dine place when in the desert.

## Relocation

The original location on Palm Canyon Drive fell victim to skyrocketing property values, but the devoted patrons – young and old alike – have flocked to the new locations on Washington Street in La Quinta. More specifically, the restaurant changed hands and locations a few times before arriving at its current spot in La Quinta in 2005.

## Current Status

Today, Louise's Pantry offers premium-quality comfort foods such as a complete selection of Succulent Burgers and Fries, a large choice of Pantry Sandwiches including hot Reuben and French Dip Sandwiches, delicious Salads like the popular Cobb Salad, a huge selection of delicious Hearty Breakfasts including Biscuits and Gravy. Since its inception in 1946, this unassuming eatery has woven itself into the fabric of desert life, becoming a cherished spot for locals and a must-visit for curious travelers.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about **Pinocchio in the Desert** in Palm Springs:

## Overview
Pinocchio in the Desert is a popular Palm Springs breakfast and lunch bistro restaurant with a warm welcoming lively fun European atmosphere. It's located in beautiful Palm Springs busy downtown tourist district adjacent to all the popular venues and shops in town.

## Breakfast Menu
The breakfast menu features all your favorite classics: Omelets and Eggs Benedict – from the classic to the indulgent crab cake or lobster benedict, Pancakes, Waffles & French Toast – golden, fluffy, and perfect with syrup or fruit.

## Signature Feature: Bottomless Champagne
Pinocchio in the Desert is renowned for its generous portions and bottomless mimosas—a local tradition that keeps guests returning again and again. They offer bottomless champagne @ $3.95, well drinks & well martini from $2.50.

## Location & Hours
The restaurant is located at 134 E Tahquitz Canyon Way, Palm Springs, CA 92262, with hours Mon-Thu 7:30 am - 3:00 pm, Fri-Sat 7:30 am - 4:00 pm, and Sun 7:30 am - 4:00 pm.

## Pricing
Most menu items are under $10, making it an affordable option for breakfast in Palm Springs.

content.text: I have a robust set. Let me return the structured output with ~12 entities spanning the valley.

entities.name: Cheeky's
entities.why_mentioned: Top-of-mind breakfast destination in Palm Springs across multiple best-of lists; Michelin Guide listing; known for signature bacon flight and weekly-changing menu; consistently cited by Eater, Locale Magazine, Tripadvisor and Yelp as a leading brunch spot

entities.name: Norma's
entities.why_mentioned: Iconic Parker Palm Springs hotel brunch destination; open-air terrace; known for signature Zillion Dollar Lobster Frittata and Chocolate Decadent French Toast; anchor reference for resort brunch in the valley

entities.name: FARM
entities.cuisine_or_type: French-American brunch (Provencal cafe, crepes, croque madame)
entities.why_mentioned: Opened 2016 in La Plaza courtyard downtown; widely cited brunch destination for French breakfast in garden setting; walk-in only breakfast/lunch; consistently appears on Locale, Visit Greater Palm Springs and Tripadvisor brunch lists

entities.name: Elmer's Restaurant
entities.why_mentioned: Palm Springs outpost of the Pacific Northwest pancake-house chain founded 1960; uses original Walt Elmer pancake batter recipe; longtime hearty-breakfast anchor on Palm Canyon Drive cited by Palm Springs Life

entities.name: Pinocchio in the Desert
entities.why_mentioned: Downtown Palm Springs breakfast institution known for bottomless mimosas and large breakfast menu (eggs Benedict variations, omelets); long-standing brunch anchor in tourist district

entities.name: Sherman's Deli & Bakery
entities.cuisine_or_type: Jewish deli & bakery (breakfast, brunch, lunch, dinner)
entities.why_mentioned: Founded 1963 by Sherman Harris; the Coachella Valley's signature Jewish deli operating 60+ years; landmark breakfast/brunch destination with locations in Palm Desert and Palm Springs; cited by Roadfood and Visit Palm Springs as longtime institution

entities.name: Keedy's Fountain & Grill
entities.why_mentioned: Opened June 30, 1957; dedicated as a Local Landmark in 1986; original Formica counter, blue vinyl chairs and fountain still in use; cornerstone Coachella Valley diner cited by Palm Springs Life

entities.name: Wilma & Frieda
entities.cuisine_or_type: Brunch / comfort food with a twist
entities.why_mentioned: El Paseo brunch destination open ~10+ years; featured on Food Network; named after owners' grandmothers; consistently cited by Palm Springs Life and Locale Magazine; second location in Palm Springs

entities.name: Louise's Pantry
entities.why_mentioned: Originally opened 1946 as a drugstore lunch counter on Palm Canyon Drive in Palm Springs; relocated to La Quinta in 2005; multi-generation valley institution still drawing devoted patrons; cited by Palm Springs Life best-of-the-best breakfast

entities.name: The Palms Cafe
entities.why_mentioned: Cornerstone Rancho Mirage breakfast spot operating 20+ years; cited as best banana walnut pancakes in the valley by local guides; anchor independent in Rancho Mirage breakfast scene

entities.name: Roost Cafe and Bistro
entities.why_mentioned: Highway 111 brunch independent; consistently cited on Rancho Mirage breakfast lists by Cactus Hugs and Wanderlog; French-inspired menu sets it apart in Rancho Mirage

entities.name: Lala's Waffles Crepes & Shakes
entities.why_mentioned: Family-owned breakfast/brunch destination on Jackson St; consistently ranked in Coachella/Indio top-10 breakfast lists by Yelp and Tripadvisor; praised for Farmer's Crepe and avocado toast; expanding presence with Cathedral City and Palm Springs locations

entities.name: Heirloom Craft Kitchen
entities.why_mentioned: Between Indio and La Quinta; cited by Visit Greater Palm Springs and AvantStay Coachella Valley restaurant guides as a fresh, dietary-inclusive brunch destination with farm-to-table menu

entities.name: Don & Sweet Sue's Cafe
entities.why_mentioned: Longtime Cathedral City diner with half-circle counter; cited by Palm Springs Life best-diners feature and Cactus Hugs Cathedral City breakfast guide as a classic, no-trends comfort breakfast institution

content.text: Returned 14 entities spanning Palm Springs, Palm Desert, Rancho Mirage, La Quinta, Indio, and Cathedral City — covering the dominant best-of names (Cheeky's, Norma's, FARM, Wilma & Frieda), longtime institutions (Keedy's 1957, Louise's Pantry 1946, Sherman's Deli 1963, Don & Sweet Sue's), hotel/resort brunch (Norma's at Parker), notable independents (Roost, Heirloom Craft Kitchen, The Palms Cafe, Pinocchio in the Desert), chain anchor (Elmer's), and an east-valley standout (Lala's Waffles). All claims (founding years, recognitions, locations) are substantiated by the listed sources.
